name: TATA AIG Scholarship
eligibility: Girl students from Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Bihar, J&K, Jharkhand, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Odisha, Punjab, Sikkim, Tripura, Uttar Pradesh & Maharashtra
Completed Class 12 from JNV, KV, or State Govt. School
Belong to SC/ST/Gen-EWS/PwD category
Cleared JEE (Main/Advanced) or NEET
Secured admission in top 200 NIRF Engineering Colleges or Government Medical Colleges (2025)
benefits: Full financial support including tuition fees, living expenses, study materials, and a one-time gift of a brand-new laptop/tablet.

name: Reliance Foundation Scholarship
eligibility: Be a resident Indian citizen
Passed Standard 12th with minimum 60%
Currently enrolled in the 1st year (academic year 2025-26) in a regular full-time degree course in any stream
Household income < Rs. 15 Lakhs (preference given to < Rs. 2.5 Lakhs)
Aptitude Test is mandatory
benefits: Up to INR 2,00,000 over the duration of the degree programme.

name: Infosys STEM Star Scholarship
eligibility: Indian female students
Completed Class 12
Enrolled in 1st-year STEM UG course (NIRF-accredited college)
2nd-year B.Arch / 5-year Integrated & Dual Degree students also eligible
Family income ≤ ₹8,00,000
benefits: Up to ₹1,00,000/year (max 4 years) covering actual expenses.

name: HDFC Bank Parivartan's ECSS Programme
eligibility: Indian nationals pursuing UG (B.Com, B.Sc, B.A, BCA, B.Tech, MBBS, LLB, B.Arch, Nursing, etc.)
Min. 55% in last exam
Family income ≤ ₹2.5L
Preference for students facing personal/family crisis in last 3 years
benefits: General UG students: INR 30,000. Professional UG students: INR 50,000.

name: Kotak Kanya Scholarship
eligibility: Open to meritorious girl students across India
Must have scored 75% or above in Class 12 (or equivalent CGPA)
Annual family income should be below ₹6,00,000
Secured admission (2025–26) to the first year of professional graduation programs (Engineering, MBBS, Integrated LLB, BS-MS/Research, Design, Architecture, etc.)
Reputed institutes such as NIRF/NAAC-accredited colleges, IISERs, or IISc Bangalore
benefits: ₹1.5 lakh per year till course completion — for tuition, hostel, internet, transport, laptop, books & stationery.

name: SBI Platinum Jubilee Asha Scholarship
eligibility: Indian Nationals
Pursuing UG (any year) in Top 300 NIRF/NAAC A+ colleges
Last year score: 75% / 7 CGPA (Relaxation: SC/ST – 67.5% / 6.3 CGPA)
Family income ≤ ₹6,00,000
50% reserved for Girls + 50% for SC/ST
benefits: Degree Students: Up to ₹75,000. IIT Students: Up to ₹2,00,000. Medical Students: Up to ₹4,50,000. IIM Students: Up to ₹5,00,000.

name: LIC Golden Jubilee Scholarship
eligibility: Passed Class 10 or 12 with minimum 60% marks (2022–25)
Enrolled in 1st year of graduation, integrated, vocational, or diploma courses
Government-recognised institution/ITI
Family income not exceeding ₹4,50,000 per annum
Special category for Girl Child: Passed Class 10 with 60%+ pursuing intermediate/vocational/diploma
benefits: MBBS/BAMS/BHMS/BDS: ₹40,000/year. B.E./B.Tech/B.Arch: ₹30,000/year. Graduation/Diploma: ₹20,000/year. Girl Child (Class 12/Diploma): ₹15,000/year.

name: Azim Premji Scholarship
eligibility: Female students only
Passed Class 10 & 12 from govt schools in eligible states
1st-year UG/Diploma (2–5 yrs) students

Eligible States: Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Jharkhand, Karnataka, Madhya Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, Odisha, Puducherry, Rajasthan, Sikkim, Telangana, Tripura, Uttar Pradesh, and Uttarakhand
benefits: ₹30,000 per year.

name: Bharti Airtel Scholarship
eligibility: Confirmed admission (2025–26) to 1st year UG/5-year integrated courses
Courses in Electronics, Telecom, IT, Computer Science, Data Science, Aerospace, or Emerging Tech (AI, IoT, AR/VR, ML, Robotics)
Top 50 NIRF engineering institutes (2024 list)
Indian citizen and resident
Family income ≤ ₹8.5 lakh/year
benefits: 100% of annual course fees for entire program (up to 5 years). Hostel & mess fees covered. Laptop provided in the first year.

name: Vahani Scholarship
eligibility: Class 12th students
Above and equal to 85% in Class 10th
Annual family income should be less than 3 lakhs
benefits: Full fee coverage (tuition + hostel/PG). Laptop (one-time), internet, travel & textbook stipends. Mentorship, career guidance, skill training, internships & workshops.

name: Foundation for Excellence (FFE)
eligibility: Family income ≤ ₹3L/year
Passed Class 12 in 2023–25
1st-year BE/B.Tech, Dual MTech, MBBS, or 5-year Law student
JEE Main ≤ 90,000 | JEE Adv ≤ 30,000 | MBBS Rank ≤ 40,000 | KCET Rank ≤ 6,000
benefits: ₹50,000 per year till course completion.

name: Aditya Birla Capital Scholarship
eligibility: Open exclusively to meritorious girl students
Minimum of 60% marks in most recent academic year
Annual family income must not exceed Rs. 6 lakh
benefits: Class 9 to 12: ₹25,000. General Graduation: ₹30,000. Professional Graduation: ₹45,000. IIT/NIT/IIM: ₹60,000.

name: Panasonic Ratti Chatr Scholarship
eligibility: Students across India who have qualified entrance test for B.E./B.Tech. programs at any IIT
Currently in their first year
More than 75% marks in Class 12
Annual family income should be INR 8,00,000 or below
benefits: INR 70,250 per year for four years.

name: Aspire Scholarship Program
eligibility: Students enrolling in 1st year of B.Tech programs at IIT Kharagpur, IIT Hyderabad, IIT Patna, IIT Delhi, IIT Bombay, IIT Goa, IIITDM Jabalpur, IIIT Delhi, IIIT Naya Raipur, Jabalpur Engineering College, NIAMT Ranchi
Annual family income must not exceed INR 6,00,000
benefits: Full scholarship based on actual fee structure. Covers tuition, hostel, food, and related academic expenses. Valid for 4 years with renewal.

name: HOPE Engineering Scholarship by Schaeffler India
eligibility: PwD female students across India
1st year of any engineering program at a state or UGC-recognised college
40% or above in Class 12 (Science) in 2024–25
Preference for Automobile, Mechanical, Mechatronics, Electrical, Electronics, CS, IT branches
Family income ≤ ₹5,00,000/year
benefits: INR 50,000 per year for four years.

name: Legrand Empowering Scholarship Program
eligibility: Open to girl students across India only
Admission in B.Tech, B.E., B.Arch., BBA, B.Com., or B.Sc. programs
Passed Class 12 in 2025–26
70% or above in both Class 10 and 12
Annual family income ≤ ₹5,00,000
benefits: Up to 60% of annual course fees (max ₹60,000 per year), awarded each year based on academic performance.

name: IDFC FIRST Bank Engineering Scholarship
eligibility: Must be an Indian citizen
Open to 1st-year B.Tech/B.E. students
Admission in one of the eligible colleges (refer website for list)
Family income ≤ ₹6,00,000 per year
Should not be receiving any other scholarship or fee waiver
benefits: A scholarship of INR 1 Lakh per year for up to four years.

name: DXC Progressing Minds Scholarship (STEM)
eligibility: Open to women & transgender students
Any year of a STEM UG course
60%+ in the last class/semester
Family income ≤ ₹4,00,000 per year
Students from all over India can apply
benefits: INR 50,000 (Fixed Amount).

name: Deutsche Bank Engineering Scholarship
eligibility: First Year Girls in Engineering Program 2025-2026
Up to 20 Years of Age
Annual Family Income Less Than INR 5 Lakhs
60% Aggregate Score in Class 10th & 12th
College Locations: Mumbai, Pune, Bengaluru, Jaipur
benefits: Up to ₹1 Lakh Tuition Fee, Laptop. Core Employability & English Communication Training, Domain Skills Training, Internship Support & Placement Assistance.

name: U-Go Scholarship Program
eligibility: Young women pursuing professional UG courses (teaching, nursing, pharmacy, medicine, engineering, architecture, law, etc.)
Any year except final year
70%+ in Class 10 & 12
Family income < ₹5,00,000 per year
Open to students across India
benefits: Teaching: Up to ₹40,000/year. Nursing & Pharma: Up to ₹40,000/year for 4 years. 3-year courses: Up to ₹40,000/year. Engineering/MBBS/Law/Architecture: Up to ₹60,000/year for 4 years.

name: L'Oréal India For Young Women In Science
eligibility: Girls only
85%+ in Class 12 (Science)
Family income ≤ ₹6,00,000 per year
Pursuing UG in a science-related field (any year except final year)
Open to students across India
benefits: Fixed scholarship of INR 62,500.

name: Colgate Keep India Smiling Scholarship (BDS)
eligibility: Open to BDS students in any year
65%+ in Class 12
2nd–4th year BDS students need 60%+ in last semester
Studying in a recognised govt./private college
Family income < ₹8,00,000 per year
Indian nationals only
benefits: One-time scholarship of INR 75,000.

name: Santoor Scholarship Programme
eligibility: Young women pursuing full-time UG courses
In Andhra Pradesh or Maharashtra only
Must be in 1st year of degree
Completed Class 12 from a govt. school/junior college
Completed Class 10 from a local govt. school
benefits: One-time scholarship amount of INR 30,000 (fixed).

name: Rolls-Royce Wings4Her Scholarship
eligibility: Female engineering students in 1st–3rd year across India
Branches: aerospace, CS, electronics, marine, etc.
AICTE-recognised institution (preference for govt. colleges)
60%+ in Class 10 & 12
Family income ≤ ₹4,00,000 per year
benefits: Selected students will receive a scholarship of INR 35,000.

name: Muthoot M George Higher Education Scholarship
eligibility: Pursuing full-time B.Tech, MBBS, or B.Sc Nursing
Admission secured for AY 2025 in a recognised institution
90%+ in Class 12
Family income ≤ ₹2,00,000 per year
Qualified the relevant entrance exam
Residents of Kerala, Karnataka, Telangana, Maharashtra, Delhi, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu
benefits: MBBS students: ₹2,40,000. B.Tech & B.Sc Nursing students: ₹1,20,000.

name: Sitaram Jindal Foundation Scholarship Scheme
eligibility: Age must be below 30 years
Open to students of Class 11, 12, ITI, Diploma, UG, or PG
General, Medical, Engineering streams
Family income up to ₹4,00,000 for salaried families
Family income up to ₹2,50,000 for others
benefits: Tuition fee support. Assistance for study material expenses. Accommodation support for hostellers.
